{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Development Representative",
  "description": "Flutterwave was founded on the principle that every African must be able to participate and thrive in the global economy. To achieve this objective, we have built a trusted payment infrastructure that allows consumers and businesses (African and International) make and receive payments in a convenient border-less manner.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Sales Development Representative
Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki,&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	We are looking for a Sales Development Representative (SDR) to join our Enterprise Sales team.
	As a SDR you will be responsible for identifying potential customers, generating new business opportunities, and managing leads through the sales pipeline and setting up our sales closers for success.
	As the first line of communication with Flutterwave&rsquo;s prospects, the ideal candidate will have a thorough understanding of the sales process and will excel at researching and qualifying leads, starting new relationships and showcasing Flutterwave&rsquo;s offerings to prospects in a persuasive way.&nbsp;
	We are looking for someone who has excellent communication skills, a strong work ethic, and a proven track record of success in sales.
	If you are self-motivated and have a passion for sales, we would love to hear from you.&nbsp;


What Your Day-to-Day Activities Will Be Like&nbsp;


	Generate leads to manage and maintain a pipeline of interested prospects to create new business opportunities for Flutterwave
	Build nurturing relationships with customers to identify their potential needs and qualify their interests and viability to drive sales
	Move solid leads through the funnel, connecting them to a salesperson, and arranging in-person meetings, emails, or phone calls
	Collaborate with the sales team to develop strategies for reaching sales targets&nbsp;
	Developing sales strategies to draw in potential buyers or to solicit new potential customers
	Following up with potential customers who expressed interest but did not purchase our services
	Identify best practices to refine Flutterwave&rsquo;s lead-generation playbook
	Stay up-to-date on market trends, competition, and industry developments
	Attend networking events and tradeshows to build relationships and generate leads
	Manage a small portfolio of existing Flutterwave Enterprise clients to support administrative functions, FX trading and also identify cross selling opportunities.&nbsp;
	Ensure personal information of customers, employees, and other individuals the company conducts business with is processed and protected in line with applicable data privacy policies, privacy laws, and global best practices.


Requirements
Required competency and skillset to be a waver


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in business, sales or a related field
	4+ years of experience as a sales development representative, with a track record of exceeding lead targets and sales quota
	Proficiency in customer relationship management (CRM) software
	Strong verbal and written communication skills
	Ability to build and maintain relationships with potential customers
	Knowledge of sales techniques and strategies
	Strong problem-solving, negotiation skills and analytical skills
	Time management and organizational skills
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team
	A desire to learn and grow within the sales organization.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-27",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-28",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Flutterwave"
    
  },

    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",

       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "196585"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Sales Development Representative Job at Flutterwave




        
    

        
        Flutterwave was founded on the principle that every African must be able to participate and thrive in the global economy. To achieve this objective, we have built a trusted payment infrastructure that allows consumers and businesses (African and International) make and receive payments in a convenient border-less manner.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Development Representative
Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a Sales Development Representative (SDR) to join our Enterprise Sales team.
	As a SDR you will be responsible for identifying potential customers, generating new business opportunities, and managing leads through the sales pipeline and setting up our sales closers for success.
	As the first line of communication with Flutterwave’s prospects, the ideal candidate will have a thorough understanding of the sales process and will excel at researching and qualifying leads, starting new relationships and showcasing Flutterwave’s offerings to prospects in a persuasive way. 
	We are looking for someone who has excellent communication skills, a strong work ethic, and a proven track record of success in sales.
	If you are self-motivated and have a passion for sales, we would love to hear from you. 


What Your Day-to-Day Activities Will Be Like 


	Generate leads to manage and maintain a pipeline of interested prospects to create new business opportunities for Flutterwave
	Build nurturing relationships with customers to identify their potential needs and qualify their interests and viability to drive sales
	Move solid leads through the funnel, connecting them to a salesperson, and arranging in-person meetings, emails, or phone calls
	Collaborate with the sales team to develop strategies for reaching sales targets 
	Developing sales strategies to draw in potential buyers or to solicit new potential customers
	Following up with potential customers who expressed interest but did not purchase our services
	Identify best practices to refine Flutterwave’s lead-generation playbook
	Stay up-to-date on market trends, competition, and industry developments
	Attend networking events and tradeshows to build relationships and generate leads
	Manage a small portfolio of existing Flutterwave Enterprise clients to support administrative functions, FX trading and also identify cross selling opportunities. 
	Ensure personal information of customers, employees, and other individuals the company conducts business with is processed and protected in line with applicable data privacy policies, privacy laws, and global best practices.


Requirements
Required competency and skillset to be a waver


	Bachelor’s degree in business, sales or a related field
	4+ years of experience as a sales development representative, with a track record of exceeding lead targets and sales quota
	Proficiency in customer relationship management (CRM) software
	Strong verbal and written communication skills
	Ability to build and maintain relationships with potential customers
	Knowledge of sales techniques and strategies
	Strong problem-solving, negotiation skills and analytical skills
	Time management and organizational skills
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team
	A desire to learn and grow within the sales organization.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Cluster Loan Officer (Trader Finance)",
  "description": "Confidence Microfinance Bank is recruiting suitable candidates o fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Cluster Loan Officer (Trader Finance)

Job Location: Kwara

About the Role


	We are hiring Cluster Loan Officers to drive our trader loan product targeted at daily-income earners (market women, POS agents, artisans, transport operators, etc.).


This is a high-impact field role responsible for:


	Sourcing qualified traders&nbsp;
	Disbursing small-ticket loans&nbsp;
	Ensuring daily/weekly repayments&nbsp;
	Managing a defined market cluster.


Key Responsibilities
Loan Origination:


	Identify and onboard traders within assigned clusters (markets, streets, hubs)&nbsp;
	Educate customers on loan products and repayment structure&nbsp;
	Build strong relationships with trader associations and market leaders&nbsp;
	Meet weekly acquisition targets&nbsp;


Collections &amp; Recovery (Most Important):


	Drive daily/weekly loan repayments&nbsp;
	Maintain repayment discipline within your cluster&nbsp;
	Follow up aggressively on overdue accounts&nbsp;


Cluster Ownership:


	Take full ownership of your assigned market/area&nbsp;
	Build a strong borrower network within your cluster&nbsp;


Performance Targets:


	Number of active borrowers onboarded monthly&nbsp;
	Portfolio size within your cluster&nbsp;
	Repayment rate.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND / BSc Degree in any discipline&nbsp;
	1 - 3 years experience in:&nbsp;
	
		Microfinance / field credit&nbsp;
		FMCG sales&nbsp;
		Agency banking / POS business&nbsp;
	
	
	Strong understanding of informal markets and trader behavior
	Ability to work on the field daily&nbsp;
	Must be street-smart, assertive, and results-driven&nbsp;
	Comfortable handling cash collections and repayment follow-ups.


Compensation


	Base Salary: N80,000 &ndash; N120,000&nbsp;


High Commission on:&nbsp;


	Loans disbursed&nbsp;
	Repayments collected&nbsp;
	Performance Bonuses for low default rates.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;support@confidencebank.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Cluster Loan Officer (Trader Finance) Job at Confidence Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        Confidence Microfinance Bank is recruiting suitable candidates o fill the position below:

Job Position: Cluster Loan Officer (Trader Finance)

Job Location: Kwara

About the Role


	We are hiring Cluster Loan Officers to drive our trader loan product targeted at daily-income earners (market women, POS agents, artisans, transport operators, etc.).


This is a high-impact field role responsible for:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Sourcing qualified traders 
	Disbursing small-ticket loans 
	Ensuring daily/weekly repayments 
	Managing a defined market cluster.


Key Responsibilities
Loan Origination:


	Identify and onboard traders within assigned clusters (markets, streets, hubs) 
	Educate customers on loan products and repayment structure 
	Build strong relationships with trader associations and market leaders 
	Meet weekly acquisition targets 


Collections & Recovery (Most Important):


	Drive daily/weekly loan repayments 
	Maintain repayment discipline within your cluster 
	Follow up aggressively on overdue accounts 


Cluster Ownership:


	Take full ownership of your assigned market/area 
	Build a strong borrower network within your cluster 


Performance Targets:


	Number of active borrowers onboarded monthly 
	Portfolio size within your cluster 
	Repayment rate.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND / BSc Degree in any discipline 
	1 - 3 years experience in: 
	
		Microfinance / field credit 
		FMCG sales 
		Agency banking / POS business 
	
	
	Strong understanding of informal markets and trader behavior
	Ability to work on the field daily 
	Must be street-smart, assertive, and results-driven 
	Comfortable handling cash collections and repayment follow-ups.


Compensation


	Base Salary: N80,000 – N120,000 


High Commission on: 


	Loans disbursed 
	Repayments collected 
	Performance Bonuses for low default rates.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Quality Engineer – Pipeline Projects (Oil & Gas Services)",
  "description": "Worknigeria was established with a Singular Vision. To help the vast Nigerian workforce find meaningful jobs. We are also on a mission to upskill them in the best ways possible and help them grow with and within their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Engineer &ndash; Pipeline Projects (Oil &amp; Gas Services)

Job Location:&nbsp;Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	The&nbsp;Quality Engineer&nbsp;will be responsible for implementing and monitoring the quality management system on pipeline projects for an oil and gas service company.
	The role ensures that all pipeline construction, installation, testing and commissioning activities comply with project specifications, industry codes/standards and client requirements.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement Project Quality Plans, Inspection &amp; Test Plans (ITPs), procedures and checklists for pipeline projects in line with ISO 9001 and relevant ASME/API standards.
	Plan and execute quality control activities on pipeline construction and installation: material receiving inspections, welding inspections, coating/painting checks, fit?up and dimensional checks, hydrotesting, pigging, reinstatement and final walk?through.
	Review and approve welding procedures (WPS/PQR), welder qualifications, NDT procedures and reports; coordinate NDT activities (RT, UT, MT, PT) with approved vendors.
	Ensure all construction and testing activities comply with applicable codes and standards (e.g. ASME B31.4/B31.8, API, company specs) and client specifications.
	Monitor contractors&rsquo; and subcontractors&rsquo; quality performance; carry out surveillance, spot checks and formal quality audits on site.
	Identify, document and manage non?conformances (NCRs); lead root cause analysis and corrective/preventive actions (CAPA) to prevent recurrence.
	Maintain proper quality documentation and records, including ITPs, checklists, calibration certificates, test reports, welding/NDT records and as?built dossiers.
	Participate in pre?inspection meetings (PIM), construction review meetings, punch list walk?downs and mechanical completion/handover processes.
	Liaise with client representatives, third?party inspectors and internal project teams (engineering, construction, HSE) on quality?related matters.
	Support continuous improvement of quality processes and promote a strong quality culture on project sites.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Mechanical Engineering (or related discipline).
	Minimum of10 years of proven experiencein quality/QAQC roles on pipeline projects within the oil and gas servicing or EPC/EPCI environment.
	Strong knowledge of pipeline construction/installation methods, welding, NDT, coating, hydrotesting and pre?commissioning activities.
	Good understanding of applicable standards and codes (e.g. ISO 9001, ASME B31.4/B31.8, API, relevant client/company specs).
	Hands?on experience in developing and implementing Project Quality Plans, ITPs and QA/QC procedures.
	Demonstrated experience managing NCRs, root cause analysis and corrective/preventive actions.
	Proficiency in MS Office and ability to work with electronic document management systems.?
	Strong communication, documentation and interpersonal skills; able to interface effectively with clients, contractors and site teams.
	Relevant professional certifications (e.g. ISO 9001 Lead Auditor, CSWIP, NDT Level II) will be an added advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@worknigeria.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Quality Engineer – Pipeline Projects (Oil & Gas Services) Job at Worknigeria




        
    

        
        Worknigeria was established with a Singular Vision. To help the vast Nigerian workforce find meaningful jobs. We are also on a mission to upskill them in the best ways possible and help them grow with and within their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Engineer – Pipeline Projects (Oil & Gas Services)

Job Location: Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Quality Engineer will be responsible for implementing and monitoring the quality management system on pipeline projects for an oil and gas service company.
	The role ensures that all pipeline construction, installation, testing and commissioning activities comply with project specifications, industry codes/standards and client requirements.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement Project Quality Plans, Inspection & Test Plans (ITPs), procedures and checklists for pipeline projects in line with ISO 9001 and relevant ASME/API standards.
	Plan and execute quality control activities on pipeline construction and installation: material receiving inspections, welding inspections, coating/painting checks, fit?up and dimensional checks, hydrotesting, pigging, reinstatement and final walk?through.
	Review and approve welding procedures (WPS/PQR), welder qualifications, NDT procedures and reports; coordinate NDT activities (RT, UT, MT, PT) with approved vendors.
	Ensure all construction and testing activities comply with applicable codes and standards (e.g. ASME B31.4/B31.8, API, company specs) and client specifications.
	Monitor contractors’ and subcontractors’ quality performance; carry out surveillance, spot checks and formal quality audits on site.
	Identify, document and manage non?conformances (NCRs); lead root cause analysis and corrective/preventive actions (CAPA) to prevent recurrence.
	Maintain proper quality documentation and records, including ITPs, checklists, calibration certificates, test reports, welding/NDT records and as?built dossiers.
	Participate in pre?inspection meetings (PIM), construction review meetings, punch list walk?downs and mechanical completion/handover processes.
	Liaise with client representatives, third?party inspectors and internal project teams (engineering, construction, HSE) on quality?related matters.
	Support continuous improvement of quality processes and promote a strong quality culture on project sites.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering (or related discipline).
	Minimum of10 years of proven experiencein quality/QAQC roles on pipeline projects within the oil and gas servicing or EPC/EPCI environment.
	Strong knowledge of pipeline construction/installation methods, welding, NDT, coating, hydrotesting and pre?commissioning activities.
	Good understanding of applicable standards and codes (e.g. ISO 9001, ASME B31.4/B31.8, API, relevant client/company specs).
	Hands?on experience in developing and implementing Project Quality Plans, ITPs and QA/QC procedures.
	Demonstrated experience managing NCRs, root cause analysis and corrective/preventive actions.
	Proficiency in MS Office and ability to work with electronic document management systems.?
	Strong communication, documentation and interpersonal skills; able to interface effectively with clients, contractors and site teams.
	Relevant professional certifications (e.g. ISO 9001 Lead Auditor, CSWIP, NDT Level II) will be an added advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Administrative Officer",
  "description": "Excel and Grace Consulting is a fast-paced business consulting organization with a track record of delivering exceptional business solutions to small and medium-scale enterprises in Nigeria, Ghana, Germany, Switzerland, Canada, etc. We are certified management consultants with the International Council of Management Consulting Institutes (CMC-Global) with over 18 years of experience. Our team of experts and consultants have authored over 20 books on business management, business structure, business operations, human resources, organizational structure, employee development, money management, and so on.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ebute-Meta, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Administrative Officer is responsible for overseeing and coordinating office operations and procedures to ensure organizational effectiveness and efficiency.
	This role includes managing office supplies, supporting staff, handling documentation, and ensuring a smooth workflow across departments.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage day-to-day administrative operations of the office
	Maintain and organize files, records, and databases (digital and physical)
	Handle incoming and outgoing correspondence (emails, phone calls, mail)
	Prepare reports, memos, letters, and other documents
	Organize meetings, appointments, and events
	Monitor and order office supplies and equipment
	Ensure the office is compliant with health, safety, and company policies
	Perform other administrative tasks as assigned by management


Requirements


	Proven experience as an Administrative Officer, Office Admin, or similar role
	Proficiency in MS Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook)
	Strong organizational and time management skills
	Excellent written and verbal communication
	Attention to detail and problem-solving ability
	Ability to work independently and handle multiple tasks
	A degree or diploma in Business Administration or related field preferred.


Salary
N150,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@eandg.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Administrative Officer Job at Excel and Grace Consulting




        
    

        
        Excel and Grace Consulting is a fast-paced business consulting organization with a track record of delivering exceptional business solutions to small and medium-scale enterprises in Nigeria, Ghana, Germany, Switzerland, Canada, etc. We are certified management consultants with the International Council of Management Consulting Institutes (CMC-Global) with over 18 years of experience. Our team of experts and consultants have authored over 20 books on business management, business structure, business operations, human resources, organizational structure, employee development, money management, and so on.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Ebute-Meta, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Administrative Officer is responsible for overseeing and coordinating office operations and procedures to ensure organizational effectiveness and efficiency.
	This role includes managing office supplies, supporting staff, handling documentation, and ensuring a smooth workflow across departments.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage day-to-day administrative operations of the office
	Maintain and organize files, records, and databases (digital and physical)
	Handle incoming and outgoing correspondence (emails, phone calls, mail)
	Prepare reports, memos, letters, and other documents
	Organize meetings, appointments, and events
	Monitor and order office supplies and equipment
	Ensure the office is compliant with health, safety, and company policies
	Perform other administrative tasks as assigned by management


Requirements


	Proven experience as an Administrative Officer, Office Admin, or similar role
	Proficiency in MS Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook)
	Strong organizational and time management skills
	Excellent written and verbal communication
	Attention to detail and problem-solving ability
	Ability to work independently and handle multiple tasks
	A degree or diploma in Business Administration or related field preferred.


Salary
N150,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
